<p>The Editor has a user accessible API that is currently in beta which can be used to help automate and extend the base functionality.</p>
<p>The API is not yet considered stable and may change in the future as we continue development. However, it is unlikely that API will change a lot from its current state.</p>
<p>The Editor API is open sourced on <a href="https://github.com/playcanvas/editor-api" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">GitHub</a>. The API documentation can be found <a href="https://api.playcanvas.com/editor/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">here</a>.</p>
<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="automation-example">Automation Example<a href="#automation-example" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Automation Example" title="Direct link to Automation Example" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p>The API can be accessed via the browser's devtools console allowing for automation of repetitive tasks. In the example below, we are using the Editor to find all the Entities that have the tag 'red' and disabling them by running code in the browser console.</p>
<p><img decoding="async" loading="lazy" width="1921" height="1080" class="img_ev3q" src="/assets/images/disable-red-boxes-3fb9269402877fa9e261309ecf89d4ae.gif"></p>
<p>Editor API code:</p>

<h2 class="anchor anchorTargetStickyNavbar_Vzrq" id="extending-editor-functionality">Extending Editor Functionality<a href="#extending-editor-functionality" class="hash-link" aria-label="Direct link to Extending Editor Functionality" title="Direct link to Extending Editor Functionality" translate="no">​</a></h2>
<p>It is possible to add extra functionality to the Editor and create custom interfaces such as adding buttons. This can be done via a number of ways such as browser extensions or <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Userscript" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">user scripts</a>.</p>
<p>User scripts is the more accessible out of the two which allows you to run custom code on top of the Editor.</p>
<p>Below we have an example where a button has been added to the <a class="" href="/user-manual/editor/interface/viewport/">Viewport</a> to generate and randomly place boxes into the scene.</p>
<p><img decoding="async" loading="lazy" width="1281" height="720" class="img_ev3q" src="/assets/images/generate-random-boxes-9c9def80c0c4b4d2dde09215203f6055.gif"></p>
<p>The PlayCanvas team are currently using the <a href="https://violentmonkey.github.io/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Violentmonkey</a> open source browser extension to manage user scripts.</p>
<p>Once the browser extension has been installed, adding your own or another developer's user script is very straightforward. Steps can be found in <a href="https://violentmonkey.github.io/guide/creating-a-userscript/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="">Violentmonkey's documentation</a>.</p>
<p>The code for the above user script is:</p>
<div class="language-javascript codeBlockContainer_Ckt0 theme-code-block" style="--prism-color: #393A34; --prism-background-color: #f6f8fa;"><div class="codeBlockContent_QJqH"><pre tabindex="0"
